title: cardamine lacustris
intro: Cardamine lacustris, also known as lake bitter-cress or watercress, is a species of flowering plant in the mustard family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia and is often found growing in damp environments such as lake shores and riverbanks. The plant has been used for medicinal purposes, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The leaves of the plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to support digestive health and provide antioxidants, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of health benefits.
